title: Decompose fractions
description: Practice decomposing fractions (breaking fractions into parts that can be added together).

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these sums equal $\dfrac{6}{3}$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
First, let's visualize $\dfrac{6}{3}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Now, let's compare each answer choice to $\dfrac{6}{3}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which fractions add together to make $\dfrac{13}{2}$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $2$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

The numerators tell us how many parts we are counting. We want $13$ equal parts.

$\blueD{1}+\greenD{4}+\purpleD{8}=13$



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{\dfrac{1}{2}}+\greenD{\dfrac{4}{2}} +\purpleD{\dfrac{8}{2}} =\dfrac{13}{2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which fractions add together to make $\dfrac{10}{4}$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $4$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

The numerators tell us how many parts we are counting. We want $10$ equal parts.

$\blueD{8}+\greenD{2}=10$


[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{\dfrac{8}{4}}+\greenD{\dfrac{2}{4}}  =\dfrac{10}{4}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which fractions add together to make $\dfrac{11}{5}$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $5$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

The numerators tell us how many parts we are counting. We want $11$ equal parts.

$\blueD{1}+\greenD{8} + \purpleD2=11$


[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{\dfrac{1}{5}}+\greenD{\dfrac{8}{5}}  + \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{5}}=\dfrac{11}{5}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which fractions add together to make $\dfrac{7}{3}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $3$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

The numerators tell us how many parts we are counting. We want $7$ equal parts.

$\blueD{1}+\greenD{4} + \purpleD2=7$



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{\dfrac{1}{3}}+\greenD{\dfrac{4}{3}}  + \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{3}}=\dfrac{7}{3}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which fractions add together to make $\dfrac{3}{6}$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $6$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

The numerators tell us how many parts we are counting. We want $3$ equal parts.

$\blueD{1}+\greenD{2}=3$



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{\dfrac{1}{6}}+\greenD{\dfrac{2}{6}}  =\dfrac{3}{6}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which fractions add together to make $\dfrac{5}{8}$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $8$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

The numerators tell us how many parts we are counting. We want $5$ equal parts.

$\blueD{4}+\greenD{1}=5$



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{\dfrac{4}{8}}+\greenD{\dfrac{1}{8}}  =\dfrac{5}{8}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete this equation.**

$\dfrac{2}{8}+ \dfrac{3}{8}+\dfrac{1}{8}+$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $= \dfrac{10}{8}$
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $8$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

[[☃ image 1]]

We already have $6$ eighths.



[[☃ image 2]]

How many more eighths do we need to make $10$ eighths?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete this equation.**

$\dfrac{4}{6}+ \dfrac{9}{6}+\dfrac{5}{6}+$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $= \dfrac{21}{6}$
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $6$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

[[☃ image 1]]

We already have $18$ sixths.


[[☃ image 2]]

How many more sixths do we need to make $21$ sixths?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete this equation.**

$\dfrac{2}{5}+ \dfrac{3}{5}+\dfrac{1}{5}+$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $= \dfrac{9}{5}$
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $5$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

[[☃ image 1]]

We already have $6$ fifths.



[[☃ image 2]]

How many more fifths do we need to make $9$ fifths?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete this equation.**

$\dfrac{4}{8}+ \dfrac{3}{8}+\dfrac{1}{8}+$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $= \dfrac{14}{8}$
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $8$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

[[☃ image 1]]

We already have $8$ eighths.



[[☃ image 2]]

How many more eighths do we need to make $14$ eighths?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete this equation.**

$\dfrac{1}{2}+ \dfrac{6}{2}+$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $= \dfrac{12}{2}$
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $2$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

[[☃ image 1]]

We already have $7$ halves.

[[☃ image 2]]

How many more halves do we need to make $12$ halves?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete this equation.**

$\dfrac{1}{3}+ \dfrac{3}{3}+\dfrac{1}{3}+$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $= \dfrac{7}{3}$
hints: All of the fractions have the same denominator, $3$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

[[☃ image 1]]

We already have $5$ thirds.


[[☃ image 2]]

How many more thirds do we need to make $7$ thirds?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each expression less than, equal to, or greater than $\dfrac{7}{10}?$**

 | | 
- | - | -
$\dfrac{3}{10} + \dfrac{4}{10}$ | [[☃ dropdown 1]] | $\dfrac{7}{10}$
$\dfrac{2}{10} + \dfrac{1}{10} + \dfrac{3}{10}$ | [[☃ dropdown 4]] | $\dfrac{7}{10}$
$\dfrac{2}{10} + \dfrac{3}{10} + \dfrac{1}{10} + \dfrac{1}{10}$ | [[☃ dropdown 5]] | $\dfrac{7}{10}$
hints:
All of the fractions have the same denominator, $10$, so the parts have the same size. We are ready to add.

Let's look at each expression.

$\dfrac{3}{8} + \dfrac{3}{8} + \dfrac{1}{8} + \dfrac{5}{8}$ | [[☃ dropdown 3]] | $\dfrac{12}{8}$
hints:
